user_query:
Conduct a complete patent novelty search for the technical solution in input/technical_solution.md.

Deliverables (write exactly to these paths under output/):
1) output/report.md — A professional novelty search report with sections I–VII as follows: I. User Solution, II. Technical Features, III. Feature Comparison Table, IV. Novelty/Inventive Step Comment, V. Novelty Search Conclusion, VI. Retrieval Strategy, VII. Related Patent List. Include on the first page the disclaimer text copied verbatim from input/disclaimer.md.

2) output/retrieval_strategy.tsv — A tab-separated table with columns: step_no, step_name, strategy, num_results. Add at least 3 retrieval steps; num_results must be integers.

3) output/patents.csv — A CSV of at least 50 related patents with columns: patent_number,title,applicant,publication_date,ipc,abstract,similarity_score,similarity_stars. Sort rows by similarity_score descending. Set similarity_stars as an integer 0–5.

4) output/comparison.json — A JSON object performing feature comparison for the top 3 patents (by similarity_score). Include:
- technical_features: an array of at least 5 items, where technical_features[0] equals "Technical Subject".
- For each feature, provide objects prior1, prior2, prior3 with fields patent_number (matching values from output/patents.csv), label (one of "Disclosed","Common General Knowledge","Not Disclosed"), and excerpt (non-empty string).

5) output/comments.md — Novelty/inventive step comments that reference the top 3 patents and contain at least one line starting with "Conclusion:" that identifies either an X Document or Y Document.

Ensure the "Related Patent List" section in output/report.md lists the same patents as output/patents.csv.
